task: Add the next Pro Rust product-core slice: a keyless server-side execution admission gate that composes workspace/tenant auth, credential-vault handle, quota reservation, and pre-execution blockers into one denied admission payload. Expose one local API endpoint, render one graph-first web panel/action, and synchronize Pro docs/evidence. This remains preview-only and must not add sessions, passwords, JWT validation, external auth, admission tokens, credential storage, provider calls, quota or billing mutation, worker execution, Redis/Postgres connections, durable intent persistence, package publishing, npm dependencies, or OSS runtime changes.
securityRequirements: Mention auth, secrets, permissions, and sensitive-data handling explicitly.
Do not accept or store passwords, tokens, provider secrets, API keys, or admission tokens.
Execution admission must stay denied until hosted tenant auth, vault-handle issuance, quota reservation, worker lease, durable intent store, and idempotent commit storage exist.
dependencyRequirements: Mention new or upgraded packages, lockfile changes, and dependency impact explicitly.
performanceRequirements: Mention performance, latency, or load impact in evidence.
understandingRequirements: Explain why this admission gate is a server-computed prerequisite checklist and not a real execution authorization token.
continuityRequirements: Reuse the existing workspace access gate, credential-vault boundary, quota-ledger boundary, pre-execution boundary, Axum endpoint style, and compact web panel patterns.
patternSummary: Reuse existing Pro domain boundary payloads, API route handlers, web fetchJson helper, and preview-only gate panels.
smallestViableChange: Add a deterministic execution admission payload that composes existing auth/vault/quota/preflight blockers while reporting admission and execution disabled.
assumptions: The Pro runtime remains local preview-only.
Real provider/search execution is still intentionally blocked.
Credential values and admission tokens are never accepted, displayed, stored, or returned by this admission gate.
acceptanceCriteria: Domain tests prove execution admission denies execution and composes auth, vault, quota, and preflight blockers.
API tests prove the endpoint returns a server-computed admission payload and no secret-shaped fields.
Web tests prove the graph-first shell contains the admission panel/action, endpoint path, and renderer.
Docs and evidence explain that this is not real auth enforcement, credential access, quota reservation, provider execution, durable queue persistence, or billing mutation.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API exposes a keyless execution admission endpoint.
Pro web shell can request and render a denied server-side admission decision for the current graph run.
riskJustification: This is a keyless local admission-check payload. It composes existing auth, vault, quota, and preflight blockers without issuing tokens, accepting credentials, reserving quota, mutating billing, starting workers, connecting Redis/Postgres, persisting intents, calling providers, or writing results.
